sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: SV Zulte Waregem are struggling, winless in their last 6 matches with 4 draws and 2 losses, though they've managed to score in most games. RAAL La Louvière show mixed form with 2 wins, 2 losses, and a draw in their last 5, but they've been competitive, scoring in 4 of those matches. Both teams have shown a tendency for both teams to score in recent games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, both matches ended in 0-0 draws, indicating tight, defensively focused games. SV Zulte Waregem have dominated possession in these matchups, but RAAL La Louvière have created more big chances, failing to convert them.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: SV Zulte Waregem, sitting 9th with 22 points, have scored 23 goals but underperformed their expected goals by -3.44. RAAL La Louvière, 14th with 18 points, have struggled offensively with only 12 goals, though their defense has been relatively solid, conceding 17. Waregem's higher possession and slightly better attacking stats give them a slight edge.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For SV Zulte Waregem, Jeppe Erenbjerg has been a standout with 7 goals, overperforming his expected goals. RAAL La Louvière will rely on Jerry Afriyie in attack, though he’s underperformed his xG of 3.35 with just 2 goals.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore's probabilities (39% home win, 34% draw, 27% away win) and Waregem's home advantage, they are slight favorites. However, La Louvière's defensive resilience and recent head-to-head draws suggest a tight contest, likely low-scoring with a chance of another stalemate.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between SV Zulte Waregem and RAAL La Louvière ended in a 1-1 stalemate at Elindus Arena. Both teams found the net, with Zulte Waregem taking the lead through a penalty converted by Anton Tanghe in the 33rd minute. However, La Louvière responded just before the break with a well-taken goal by Pape Fall in the 45th minute. The scoreline reflects a balanced encounter so far, with each side showing moments of quality despite differing approaches to the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: SV Zulte Waregem dominated possession in the first half, controlling the ball for the majority of the time. They created more opportunities in the final third, frequently entering the opposition’s penalty area and generating a couple of notable chances. However, their finishing let them down at times, missing a big opportunity to extend their lead. Defensively, they were solid but not impenetrable, as La Louvière managed to capitalize on a late chance. Anton Tanghe stood out with his composure from the spot, while the team showed intent to push forward consistently.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: RAAL La Louvière, on the other hand, played a more conservative game, ceding possession to Zulte Waregem for much of the half. Despite this, they were effective in their limited attacking forays, with Pape Fall’s goal showcasing their ability to strike on the counter or from quick transitions. Their defensive structure held firm under pressure, and they were more successful in physical battles, winning a higher percentage of duels. They’ll be pleased to go into the break level, but they’ll need to create more openings if they want to take something from this game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The attacking momentum in the first half was heavily in favor of Zulte Waregem for long stretches, as they maintained consistent pressure with several dangerous spells in the opposition half. Early on, they set the tone with sustained attacks, building momentum through the middle of the half with waves of forward play, particularly around the time of their goal. La Louvière, however, showed resilience with a notable shift in momentum during a brief period midway through the half, culminating in their equalizer just before the break. This late surge could give them confidence heading into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: In the second half, Zulte Waregem will likely look to capitalize on their dominance in possession and final third entries to create clearer chances. Converting big opportunities will be crucial for them to secure the win. For La Louvière, maintaining their physical edge in duels and exploiting quick transitions could be the key to an upset. Their ability to defend compactly while striking on the break will be tested against a home side eager to push for a victory. Both teams have shown they can score, so discipline at the back will be vital.
